description: Auto-Wrap monitors only CFA flows — GDA flows are invisible to it
prompt: I set up Auto-Wrap for my account but my balance keeps draining to zero even though I have underlying tokens. I'm distributing via a GDA pool. Why isn't Auto-Wrap kicking in?

criteria.description: Explains Auto-Wrap only monitors CFA net flow rate (cfaV1.getNetFlow), not GDA flows
Notes GDA was introduced after Auto-Wrap was built (legacy limitation)
Does not fabricate workaround functions or GDA-aware auto-wrap features

golden_facts: Auto-Wrap uses cfaV1.getNetFlow — only CFA flows are considered
GDA was introduced after Auto-Wrap, so GDA flows are a blind spot
If distributing exclusively through GDA pools, Auto-Wrap never triggers
anti_patterns: Suggesting Auto-Wrap supports GDA flows
Fabricating a GDA-aware auto-wrap function or configuration

description: Pool membership is unlimited, but connected pools (auto-reflected balance) capped at 256 per account per token
prompt: Is there a limit to how many GDA pools an account can be a member of? I'm building a system where users subscribe to many pools. Will their balance always reflect distributions from all pools?

criteria.description: Mentions the 256 connected pool limit per account per token
Distinguishes connected pools (balance auto-reflected) from unconnected membership (must claimAll to receive)
Notes gas cost of balanceOf/realtimeBalanceOf scales linearly with connected pools
Does not fabricate configuration options to increase the limit

golden_facts: Pool membership is unlimited — an account can hold units in any number of pools
Connected pools (auto-reflected in SuperToken balance) are capped at 256 per account per token (SlotsBitmapLibrary bitmap)
Unconnected pool members must call claimAll() to receive their tokens
Gas cost of balanceOf/realtimeBalanceOf scales linearly with connected pools
anti_patterns: Saying there is no limit on pool connections
Conflating pool membership with pool connection
Fabricating a way to increase the 256 limit

description: SuperToken.upgrade() amount is always in 18 decimals regardless of underlying
prompt: I want to wrap 100 USDC into USDCx. USDC has 6 decimals. What value do I pass to SuperToken.upgrade()?

criteria.description: States the amount must be in 18-decimal SuperToken units (100e18 or 100 * 10^18)
Notes the contract handles downscaling to underlying decimals internally
Notes ERC-20 approval on the underlying must use the underlying's native decimals (approve 100e6 for USDC)
Does not say to pass the amount in underlying decimals (100e6)

golden_facts: upgrade() amount is always in SuperToken decimals (18), regardless of underlying token decimals
Pass 100 * 10^18 (not 100 * 10^6) to wrap 100 USDC
The contract handles downscaling internally when pulling from the underlying via transferFrom
ERC-20 approval on the underlying must use the underlying's native decimals (approve 100e6 for USDC, then upgrade(100e18))
anti_patterns: Saying to pass the amount in underlying token decimals (100e6 for USDC)
Confusing upgrade amount with ERC-20 transfer amount

description: distributeFlow rounding causes loss; difference goes to admin as adjustment flow
prompt: I called distributeFlow with a rate of 1000 wei/sec but my pool has 7 members with 1 unit each. Will each member get exactly 1000/7 per second?

criteria.description: Explains per-unit rate is integer division (requestedFlowRate / totalUnits), causing rounding loss
States the rounding difference goes to the pool admin as an adjustment flow
Notes that if requestedFlowRate < totalUnits, per-unit rate truncates to 0
Does not claim members receive exactly proportional amounts

golden_facts: Per-unit rate = requestedFlowRate / totalUnits (integer division, rounds down)
With 1000 / 7 = 142 wei/sec per member, 6 wei/sec is lost to rounding
The rounding difference becomes an adjustment flow to the pool admin
Pools hold no balance — tokens flow through directly to members
For instant distribute(), rounding remainder stays with the distributor (actual < requested)
If requestedFlowRate < totalUnits, per-unit rate truncates to 0 and the entire flow goes to admin
anti_patterns: Claiming members receive exactly proportional amounts
Claiming pools accumulate or hold rounding dust as balance
Not mentioning the adjustment flow to admin

description: CFASuperAppBase uses APP_LEVEL_FINAL — cannot call downstream Super Apps
prompt: I have a Super App built with CFASuperAppBase. In its callback, I want to call another Super App downstream. Is this possible?

criteria.description: States CFASuperAppBase uses APP_LEVEL_FINAL which blocks downstream Super App calls
Mentions using APP_LEVEL_SECOND and host.allowCompositeApp(targetApp) as the solution
Does not fabricate a setAppLevel function or configuration on CFASuperAppBase

golden_facts: CFASuperAppBase always uses APP_LEVEL_FINAL
FINAL apps cannot call other Super Apps downstream
To compose with other Super Apps, use APP_LEVEL_SECOND and call host.allowCompositeApp(targetApp)
anti_patterns: Saying CFASuperAppBase supports calling downstream Super Apps out of the box
Fabricating a configuration method to change the app level on CFASuperAppBase

description: SuperTokenV1Library convenience functions use address(this), not msg.sender — prank doesn't work
prompt: I'm writing Foundry tests for my Superfluid integration. I use vm.prank(alice) then call token.flow(receiver, flowrate) from the SuperTokenV1Library, but the sender is my test contract, not Alice. What's wrong?

criteria.description: Explains these functions use address(this) as sender, not msg.sender, so vm.prank doesn't affect the sender
Suggests using CFA/GDA-specific functions with explicit sender parameter for Foundry tests
Does not fabricate undocumented prank workarounds or wrapper functions (note: cache-warming gotcha IS documented)

golden_facts: SuperTokenV1Library flow/flowX/transferX use address(this) as sender — not overridable by prank
Use CFA/GDA-specific functions with explicit sender for Foundry tests
The WithCtx variants are for Super App callbacks, not for testing

The library caches Host/CFA/GDA addresses on first use — if cache is cold, prank can be consumed by the cache-warming call (token.getHost()), not the actual agreement call. This is documented in SuperTokenV1Library.abi.yaml.
anti_patterns: Suggesting vm.prank should work with these convenience functions
Not mentioning the address(this) behavior

description: Third parties can only delete CFA flows if sender is critical or jailed
prompt: Can anyone delete a Superfluid CFA stream, or only the sender and receiver? What happens during liquidation?

criteria.description: Explains third-party callers only succeed if sender is critical (negative available balance) or either party is jailed
Mentions that reward distribution during liquidation depends on the solvency period timing
Does not fabricate specific liquidation reward amounts or fixed periods

golden_facts: Sender, receiver, and flow operators can always delete a flow
Third-party callers only succeed if the sender is critical (negative available balance) or either party is jailed
Reward distribution during liquidation depends on the solvency period timing
anti_patterns: Saying only sender and receiver can ever delete a flow
Saying anyone can delete any flow at any time

description: FluidLocker instant unlock (unlockPeriod=0) triggers 80% penalty + ETH fee
prompt: I want to unlock my staked FLUID tokens immediately. What happens if I set unlockPeriod to 0?

criteria.description: States unlockPeriod=0 triggers 80% penalty — only 20% transferred immediately, 80% redistributed to stakers/LPs
Notes the 0.0001 ETH msg.value requirement (UNLOCKING_FEE)
Mentions 7-365 day range as alternative with proportional tax via Fontaine
Does not fabricate penalty percentages or fee amounts

golden_facts: unlockPeriod=0 triggers instant unlock with 80% penalty
Only 20% transferred immediately, 80% redistributed to stakers/LPs
unlock is payable — msg.value must equal 0.0001 ETH (UNLOCKING_FEE sent to DAO_TREASURY)
7-365 day periods deploy a Fontaine beacon proxy that streams SUP over the unlock period with proportional tax
anti_patterns: Saying instant unlock has no penalty
Not mentioning the ETH fee requirement

description: A GDA pool cannot be a member of another pool — no hierarchical/nested distribution
prompt: I want to build a hierarchical distribution system where a top-level GDA pool distributes to sub-pools, and each sub-pool distributes to end users. Can a pool be a member of another pool?

criteria.description: States pools cannot be members of other pools
References SUPERFLUID_POOL_NO_POOL_MEMBERS error or the explicit protocol restriction
Does not fabricate a workaround that enables pool nesting

golden_facts: A pool cannot be a member of another pool — updateMemberUnits reverts with SUPERFLUID_POOL_NO_POOL_MEMBERS
Pool addresses also cannot be pool admins (GDA_ADMIN_CANNOT_BE_POOL)
Pools cannot connect to other pools (GDA_CANNOT_CONNECT_POOL)
anti_patterns: Saying pools can be nested or used as members of other pools
Fabricating a mechanism to enable hierarchical pool distribution
